RT @mdavidallen: @mrejfox identifying a problem and realizing people care about it isn't the same thing as a solution but it's a necessary…

@jstn @jacksondame These things are all related- there is an infusion of people who historically care very little about the marginalized and will pump and extract money from any resource now. I am pro-crypto in all forms and want to find the people fighting climate change & that Wall St. culture.

@jstn @jacksondame What I would love is “here is what we are doing” and “these are some of the things we have learned” but I just get tone-policing and dancing which doesn’t exactly reduce my skepticism. It just seems like more long-form dismissiveness, thus my frustration.
Look! A good non-dismissive answer to that criticism! https://t.co/gUw4WWrjPt

@jstn @jacksondame It seems, from a relatively distant observer, that things went from “we are trying to find solutions” to “everything is bad anyway, and we are only 1/xth bad of other bad things” or even worse “I have seen numbers so big I don’t care anymore” and I would legit like to know
@jacksondame Got it. Good luck with all that.

@jacksondame Ok. I am sorry that I made you feel bad in some way. That said, these are the questions people are asking and expecting people to answer. Dismissing them because the questions make you feel uncomfortable seems unwise. I would look to having good answers instead.
@jacksondame Not a single question answered. :(
@jacksondame Wanna share any of your learnings? Did any of the things you pushed for succeed? Did any of your questions about carbon footprint get answered?

@jacksondame In response to ecological impact criticism:

Feb: "I can't find solid answers" about carbon footprint
Mar: "Many of us are pushing for change and people think we don't care for the climate"
...
Oct: "NFTs & Crypto are a scapegoat for the powerless" 

What happened in that time? https://t.co/cWUmOEH8xX
@jacksondame ok, wanna clarify?

I love how the response to criticism about the ecological impact of crypto has evolved from "we're working on proof of stake, we are working hard to make this better" to a simple "fuck off" now - that's nice https://t.co/hpOFVVnZeV

the closest thing I have found to the current NFT / Crypto culture (not it's potential, how it is right now) is money-game, like sneaker resellers or airline mile hackers or fantasy football, and cartoon avatars are a cool way to disguise how very very white
at some point, people went to the desert and lit shit on fire to avoid them, and then they found out about it and made it theirs, v strange
NFT and Crypto, like tech, now filled with vanilla acronym boys who would be down at Wall St. doing coke if this was the 80s, yuck
Bed time for me indeed.
